Write clearly and concisely to keep your reader engaged.
Vary your sentence lengths to create a natural rhythm in your writing.
Show rather than tell by using specific details and sensory descriptions.
Active voice makes your writing more direct and dynamic.
Edit ruthlessly to remove unnecessary words and fluff from your sentences.
Choose strong verbs instead of relying on weak verbs and adverbs.
Ensure your paragraph has a single, clear topic sentence.
Read your writing aloud to find awkward phrasing and flow issues.
Connect your sentences with logical transitions to guide the reader.
End your paragraph with a strong concluding sentence that summarizes your point.
